risks: Single-region deployment with no zone redundancy — Cosmos DB has isZoneRedundant=false and Storage uses Standard_LRS, leaving the system vulnerable to availability-zone failures
Public network access enabled on ML workspace (publicNetworkAccess=Enabled) and Cosmos DB (publicNetworkAccess=Enabled), exposing data and management planes to the internet
No private endpoints or VNet integration configured for any resource, limiting network-level isolation
Storage account and Cosmos DB do not explicitly disable key-based access; best practices recommend RBAC-only data-plane authentication
Basic ACR SKU has a 10 GiB storage limit and no private link support — may be insufficient as custom inference images grow
Managed online endpoint and deployment resources are not included in the plan; these are typically created post-workspace via ML CLI/SDK but represent a gap in end-to-end provisioning
correctionsRecommended: Enable zone redundancy on the Cosmos DB location (set isZoneRedundant=true) for production high availability
Upgrade Storage Account SKU from Standard_LRS to Standard_ZRS for zone-resilient blob storage

Enable managed VNet isolation on the ML workspace (set managedNetwork.isolationMode to AllowInternetOutbound or AllowOnlyApprovedOutbound) and add private endpoints for Cosmos DB, Key Vault, and Storage
Disable local/key-based authentication on the Storage Account (set allowSharedKeyAccess=false) and Cosmos DB (set disableLocalAuth=true) to enforce RBAC-only access
Upgrade ACR to Standard or Premium SKU if image storage exceeds 10 GiB or if private link is required
Consider adding a second Cosmos DB read region for geo-redundancy and improved read latency
Add explicit Cosmos DB database and container resources to the plan for complete end-to-end provisioning
